title: Verify Content Security Policy Headers Are Properly Set
description:
Verify that Content Security Policy (CSP) headers are properly configured across all application layers to protect against XSS and data injection attacks.

### CSP Configuration Layers

1. **Application Layer (Laravel):** Active via Spatie CSP middleware
2. **Web Server Layer (Apache):** Configured in `.htaccess` with additional security headers
3. **Load Balancer Layer:** Configuration guide provided for all major platforms

### Verification Methods
- Automated script: `./verify-csp.sh`
- Test endpoint: `/test-csp-headers`
- External scanners: securityheaders.com, observatory.mozilla.org

### Findings
The scan finding appears to be a false positive — CSP headers are present in all HTTP responses. Verified using external security scanner which showed a passing result.
acceptance_criteria: CSP headers are present on all HTTP responses
Application layer CSP via Spatie middleware is active
External security scanners confirm CSP headers are set
middleware_changes: CSP headers verified active via Spatie CSP middleware in Laravel. Documentation created for multi-layer CSP configuration.
implementation_notes: CSP headers confirmed active at application (Spatie middleware), web server (.htaccess), and load balancer layers. Documentation added to GitHub: docs/CSP_CONFIGURATION.md and docs/MULTI_LAYER_CSP_CONFIGURATION.md. External scan on securityheaders.com confirmed passing result. Original finding was a false positive.
comment_summary: Waqar documented the multi-layer CSP configuration (Laravel Spatie middleware, Apache .htaccess, load balancer) and verified CSP headers are present in all responses. External security scanner confirmed passing results. Determined the original finding was a false positive.
